title: geum scottsbergianum
intro: Geum scottsbergianum, also known as the New Zealand geum or Scott's geum, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae, native to New Zealand. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are thought to be due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. Geum scottsbergianum can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of ailments.
